evals.prompt: I have a Java service class that has zero comments. Can you add docstrings to it? The file is at /tmp/comment-eval-1/UserService.java
evals.expected_output: UserService.java with Javadoc docstrings added to all public methods, the class itself, and public fields. No existing code modified.
assertions: All public methods in UserService.java have a Javadoc comment (/** ... */) immediately before them
The class declaration has a Javadoc comment describing its responsibility
Public fields have Javadoc comments
No existing code lines were modified or removed — only docstrings were added
@param tags are present for methods with non-trivial parameters
@return tags are present for methods with non-void return types
Docstrings describe what the method does, not just restate the method name

evals.prompt: My Java project at /tmp/comment-eval-2/myapp has barely any comments and I need to document it for our internal AI tooling. Please add docstrings to the whole thing.
evals.expected_output: All undocumented Java files in the project have Javadoc added to classes, methods, and public fields. codeskel scan is run first, then files are processed in dependency order.
assertions: codeskel scan is invoked on the project root before processing files
Files are processed in order (leaf dependencies before dependents)
All public/protected methods across all files have Javadoc comments
All classes have class-level Javadoc describing their role
Progress is reported per file (e.g. [1/N] filename)
A summary is printed at the end with count of files and items documented
The user is reminded to run git diff to review changes

evals.prompt: hey can you add docstrings to my python module? its at /tmp/comment-eval-3/data_pipeline.py — its pretty complex with no comments at all
evals.expected_output: data_pipeline.py with Google-style docstrings added to all functions and the module-level classes. Args/Returns/Raises sections included where applicable.
assertions: All functions in data_pipeline.py have Google-style docstrings (Args:/Returns:/Raises: sections)
Classes have class-level docstrings
Public fields/class attributes that are non-obvious have docstrings
Docstrings use triple-quoted strings correctly ("""...""")
Args section lists each parameter with its type and meaning
Returns section describes the return value
No existing code was modified — only docstrings inserted
